title: Size of a Signal
summary: This page introduces measures of signal size, defining signal energy Ex as the integral of |x(t)|^2 over all time, which avoids cancellation issues from positive and negative areas. It then introduces signal power Px as the time-averaged energy, applicable when signal amplitude does not approach zero as |t| approaches infinity and energy is therefore infinite. Both measures are presented with their mathematical definitions for general and real-valued signals.
